ddls:
CREATE TABLE "election_cycles" (
  "cycle_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election cycle */,
  "cycle_name" TEXT /* Name of the election cycle (e.g., 2020 Presidential Election) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the election cycle */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the election cycle */,
  "election_type" TEXT /* Type of election (e.g., presidential, midterm, gubernatorial) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cycle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "races" (
  "race_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race */,
  "cycle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election cycle the race belongs to */,
  "branch" TEXT /* Branch of government (e.g., Governor, Congress) */,
  "race_name" TEXT /* Name of the race (e.g., AK-G1) */,
  "office" TEXT /* Office being contested (e.g., Governor, Senator) */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the race is taking place */,
  "district" INTEGER /* Congressional district number (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_races_cycle_id FOREIGN KEY ("cycle_id") REFERENCES election_cycles ("cycle_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidates" (
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each candidate */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the race the candidate is participating in */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the candidate */,
  "party" TEXT /* Party affiliation of the candidate */,
  "incumbent" INTEGER /* Whether the candidate is an incumbent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecasting model */,
  "cycle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election cycle the model is associated with */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecasting model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the forecasting model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of forecasting model (e.g., classic, advanced) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the race being forecasted */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model used */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date the forecast was made */,
  "democrat_win_probability" REAL /* Probability of the Democrat winning */,
  "republican_win_probability" REAL /* Probability of the Republican winning */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., Safe R, Lean R) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "race_categories" (
  "race_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each race category */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the race */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the category */,
  "category_date" TEXT /* Date the category was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("race_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_race_categories_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_race_categories_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "election_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each election result */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the race */,
  "democrat_won" INTEGER /* Whether the Democrat won the race (1) or not (0) */,
  "republican_won" INTEGER /* Whether the Republican won the race (1) or not (0) */,
  "uncalled" INTEGER /* Whether the race is uncalled (1) or not (0) */,
  "democrat_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes for the Democrat */,
  "republican_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes for the Republican */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "polls" (
  "poll_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each poll */,
  "race_id" INTEGER /* ID of the race being polled */,
  "poll_date" TEXT /* Date the poll was conducted */,
  "democrat_support" REAL /* Level of support for the Democrat */,
  "republican_support" REAL /* Level of support for the Republican */,
  "pollster" TEXT /* Name of the pollster */,
  "sample_size" INTEGER /* Sample size of the poll */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("poll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polls_race_id FOREIGN KEY ("race_id") REFERENCES races ("race_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, administrator) */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, logout) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the version was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_versions" (
  "forecast_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast version */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_versions_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_versions_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "candidate_forecasts" (
  "candidate_forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each candidate forecast */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast */,
  "candidate_id" INTEGER /* ID of the candidate */,
  "win_probability" REAL /* Probability of the candidate winning */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("candidate_forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_candidate_forecasts_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_candidate_forecasts_candidate_id FOREIGN KEY ("candidate_id") REFERENCES candidates ("candidate_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pollster_ratings" (
  "pollster_rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pollster rating */,
  "poll_id" INTEGER /* ID of the poll */,
  "pollster_name" TEXT /* Name of the pollster */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating of the pollster */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pollster_rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pollster_ratings_poll_id FOREIGN KEY ("poll_id") REFERENCES polls ("poll_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "model_performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model performance entry */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecasting model */,
  "cycle_id" INTEGER /* ID of the election cycle */,
  "accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model */,
  "mean_squared_error" REAL /* Mean squared error of the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_cycle_id FOREIGN KEY ("cycle_id") REFERENCES election_cycles ("cycle_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES forecast_models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "polls_data_sources" (
  "polls_data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each poll-data source link */,
  "poll_id" INTEGER /* ID of the poll */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("polls_data_source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polls_data_sources_poll_id FOREIGN KEY ("poll_id") REFERENCES polls ("poll_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_polls_data_sources_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("data_source_id")
)
